targets.content: 30% reduction in energy sector below 2030 BAU scenario
By 2030, 10% improvement in transport (land and marine) energy efficiency

Electric Vehicles (e-Mobility): by 2030, (a) Introduce Electric Vehicles (e-buses) for public transportation (10% of total Public Buses); (b) Introduce Electric Cars (e-Cars) in Vanuatu (10% of government fleet); and(c) 1000 Electric Two wheelers (e-bikes) /Three Wheelers (e-rickshaw)
By 2030, 20 % Bio-diesel (bio-fuel) Blending in Diesel
By 2030, Milage and Emission Standards for Vehicles

By 2030, Renewable Energy Capacity Addition and substituting (replacement) of fossil fuels with Coconut (Copra) Oil based Electricity Generation: transitioning to close to 100% renewable energy in the electricity generation sector
By 2030, 10% improvement in transport (land and marine) energy efficiency.

Electric Vehicles (e-mobility): by 2030, (a) Introduce e-buses for public transportation (10% of total public buses); (b) Introduce e-cars in Vanuatu (10% of government fleet); and (c) 1000 electric two wheelers (e-bikes)/three wheelers (e-rickshaw).

Vanuatu’s own pathway of sustained net negative emissions in this LEDS demonstrates the commitment needed at a global level

targets.year: Already achieved and commit to maintain
targets.content: Vanuatu’s own pathway of sustained net negative emissions in this LEDS demonstrates the commitment needed at a global level
around 50% reduction below BAU by 2050 (Figure 19)
Cumulatively, this NDC 3.0 will see emissions from electricity generation reduced by 715 kt CO₂ eq overall.
Overall, this NDC 3.0 will, if fully implemented, reduce emissions from all sectors from 2025-2035 by 1,608.57 kt CO₂ eq by 2035.

In keeping with the global stocktake outcome to triple renewable energy and double energy efficiency, Vanuatu’s NDC 3.0 mitigation contribution would reduce business as usual (BAU) emissions in the electricity sub-sector by 100% and in the energy sector overall by 30%.
Transport Emissions Reductions by 2035: 312,6 kt CO₂ eq compared to BAU

Vanuatu commits to a 10% improvement in transport (land and marine) energy efficiency, including tourism transport, by reducing fuel consumption and more efficient use of petrol (gasoline), diesel, Aviation Gasoline (AVG) and Jet Kerosene, thereby reducing emissions by 98.6 kt CO₂ eq overall.
Vanuatu commits to continue its engagement with the Pacific Blue Shipping Partnership (PBSP) pursuing a commitment of a 100% carbon-free maritime transport sector by 2050

Vanuatu commits to expanding the access to and use of Electric Vehicles (e-mobility): by 2035 (a) Introduce e-buses for public transportation (10% of total public buses); (b) Introduce e-cars in Vanuatu (10% of government fleet); and (c) 1,000 electric two wheelers (e-bikes)/three wheelers (e-rickshaw), thereby reducing emissions by 26.1 kt CO₂ eq overall
Vanuatu commits to increase the blending of diesel with biodiesel (including coconut biofuel) for use in transportation to more than 20% by 2035, thereby reducing emissions by 185 kt CO₂ eq overall.

Vanuatu commits to ensure the delivery of essential services (emergency, security, health, nutrition, education, infrastructure, WASH services, energy, transportation, telecommunications and ICT) to all people affected by climate displacement, including migrants and host communities.

measures.quote: By 2030, 10% improvement in transport (land and marine) energy efficiency
Milage and Emission Standards for Vehicles

Electric Vehicles (e-Mobility): by 2030, (a) Introduce Electric Vehicles (e-buses) for public transportation (10% of total Public Buses); (b) Introduce Electric Cars (e-Cars) in Vanuatu (10% of government fleet); and(c) 1000 Electric Two wheelers (e-bikes) /Three Wheelers (e-rickshaw)
Develop national vehicle and fuel standards (2025)

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Development of a national transport sector policy and accompanying institutional arrangements (2025-2030)
Industry skills development and capacity building within the electric transport sector (2025-2040)

Vanuatu commits to expanding emissions free and active transport such as safe walking, cycling and micro-mobility through promotion and active facilitation of healthy lifestyles and health-seeking behaviours.
Vanuatu commits to fully implementing and enforcing the Fuel and Vehicle Emission Standards
transport (land and marine) energy efficiency, including tourism transport, by reducing fuel consumption and more efficient use of petrol (gasoline), diesel,

expanding the access to and use of Electric Vehicles (e-mobility): by 2035 (a) Introduce e-buses for public transportation (10% of total public buses); (b) Introduce e-cars in Vanuatu (10% of government fleet); and (c) 1,000 electric two wheelers (e-bikes)/three wheelers (e-rickshaw),
increase the blending of diesel with biodiesel (including coconut biofuel)
Vanuatu will establish a policy and regulatory framework for the reduction of GHG in the maritime transport sector and transition all major ports to Green Ports with high resilience.
Vanuatu will establish a policy and regulatory framework for the reduction of GHG in the maritime transport sector and transition all major ports to Green Ports with high resilience.

adaptation.quote: Vanuatu commits to assessing, for all new government infrastructure investments, the “Resilience of Project Assets to Climate Change and Natural Disaster Risk”, and build to high climate change and disaster risk reduction standards, with backup, failover, low vulnerability location, etc.

Vanuatu commits to ensuring that the design and construction of public and other major infrastructure and development projects consider current and projected risks to minimise loss and damage, especially by developing and adhering to climate-proofed building codes, environmental impact assessments, regulations and development guidelines.
Vanuatu will establish a policy and regulatory framework for the reduction of GHG in the maritime transport sector and transition all major ports to Green Ports with high resilience.
